+{"id":"bd-1sip4","title":"[HIGH] ffs-tui lifetime syntax warning keeps workspace clippy red","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` stops in `ffs-tui` on a warning promoted to error.

## Failure
- File: `crates/ffs-tui/src/lib.rs:474`
- Item: `fn build_system_health_text(snap: &DashboardSnapshot) -> Text`
- Lint: `mismatched-lifetime-syntaxes`
- Suggested fix from compiler: return `Text<'_>` instead of bare `Text`

## Why this is high severity
This single signature keeps the whole workspace clippy gate red under `-D warnings`, so no one can currently use the mandated workspace lint gate as a merge signal.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Apply the explicit lifetime to the `Text` return type and rerun workspace clippy to ensure there are no additional red-gate warnings behind it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:06.552487169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:06.552487169Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","ffs-tui","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

Wire short default runs (500 ops fsx, 20 schedules crash) as ignored test functions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:15.035188728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:48.063283790Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:48.063262169Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: crash_replay_suite_short_integration (20 schedules) and fsx_stress_short_integration (500 ops) wired as ignored test functions in crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs lines 496-527","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1t0u","title":"Add edge-case unit tests for repair storage and recovery modules","description":"# Add edge-case unit tests for repair storage and recovery modules

## GOAL
Increase test coverage for the two weakest modules in ffs-repair:
- storage.rs: 5 tests / 974 LOC (1:195 ratio)
- recovery.rs: 6 tests / 915 LOC (1:153 ratio)

## TEST TARGETS

### storage.rs
- Dual-slot descriptor switchover, torn write recovery, generation monotonicity
- Raw vs packed symbol modes, CRC validation round-trips
- Layout validation for edge-case block group sizes

### recovery.rs
- Partial recovery, full corruption, noop recovery
- Evidence record emission, post-repair verification failure

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- storage.rs: ≥12 tests (from 5), recovery.rs: ≥12 tests (from 6)
- All pass, clippy clean","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-18T07:51:00.490788012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T07:58:08.119312106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T07:58:08.119244249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quality","repair","test"]}
@@ -547,6 +548,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-anfg0","title":"Expand ext4 dir block checksum tests beyond round-trip + no-panic","description":"crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs has stamp/verify_dir_block_checksum but the proptest coverage in the same file is limited to:

  - ext4_proptest_verify_dir_block_checksum_no_panic (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_verify_dir_block_checksum_short_rejects (negative)
  - ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_stamp_verify (round-trip)

None of these exercise the per-inode seed mixing — i.e. the kernel's defence against:
  - replaying a valid dir block under a different (ino, generation) tuple
  - using a stamp produced under one filesystem csum_seed on another filesystem instance
  - flipping a bit in the directory entry payload after a valid stamp

Apply the same four metamorphic relations the inode checksum harness now covers:
  - MR-INO: identical body, two different ino numbers → different stored checksums
  - MR-GEN: identical body+ino, different generation → different stored checksums
  - MR-SEED: identical body+ino+gen, different csum_seed → different stored checksums
  - MR-DETECT: single-bit flip in the covered region [0..bs-12] invalidates verification

Land them as proptests in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs alongside the existing ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_stamp_verify.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-frankenfs-pane","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:12:22.264599421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:18:57.022680334Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T22:18:57.020390690Z","close_reason":"Added MR-INO, MR-GEN, MR-SEED, MR-DETECT proptests for dir block checksum, mirroring the inode-checksum template from bd-z75f6. All 5 ext4_proptest_dir_block_checksum_* cases pass; clippy clean. sha=eaac860","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-aw9l8","title":"[MEDIUM] vendored fuser dead-code warnings are leaking into workspace gates","description":"# Review finding

Both workspace gates emit warnings from the vendored `fuser` dependency before project crates run. The current run surfaced dead-code warnings in:
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/fuse_abi.rs`
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/request.rs`

## Warnings seen in current run
- `cuse_init_in` is never constructed
- `cuse_init_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_ioctl_iovec` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_in` is never constructed
- method `unrestricted` is never used

## Why this matters
These are not the current hard stop because `ffs-tui` fails clippy first, but they still pollute the mandated workspace `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` output. Once the `ffs-tui` lifetime issue is fixed, this vendored warning inventory may become the next gate blocker under `-D warnings`.

## Repro
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Decide whether the vendored `fuser` code should carry explicit `#[allow(dead_code)]` for intentionally-unused ABI structs, or whether the vendored revision should be adjusted so workspace warning hygiene remains stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:39.543516661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:39.543516661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuser","qa","review","vendor","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-b25w4","title":"add mounted-path E2E xattr coverage for btrfs RW operations","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e.rs has extensive ext4 xattr mounted-path tests (py_setxattr, getxattr, listxattr) but btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage is limited. The unit tests exist in ffs-core but real FUSE round-trip proof is missing.

Scope:
- Add btrfs_rw_setxattr_getxattr E2E test in fuse_e2e.rs
- Test user.* namespace set/get/list cycle over mounted btrfs FUSE path
- Test security.* namespace if permissions allow
- Verify Create/Replace mode semantics match ext4 behavior
- Add btrfs_rw_removexattr E2E test for xattr deletion
- Emit SCENARIO_RESULT markers for CI visibility

Acceptance:
- At least 3 mounted-path btrfs xattr scenarios with deterministic assertions
- Tests soft-skip gracefully if mount fails (CI-safe)
- SCENARIO_RESULT markers in logs prove coverage in CI artifacts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane-3-gemini-reviewer","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:16.691939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:10.578466659Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:10.575667781Z","close_reason":"Added SCENARIO_RESULT markers to all 8 pre-existing btrfs rw xattr E2E tests (set_get_list_remove, multiple_attributes, on_directory, create_existing→EEXIST, replace_missing→ENODATA, overwrite_value, get_nonexistent, remove_nonexistent) with btrfs_rw_xattr_<name> ids + invariant-specific detail strings. Markers emit only when the test actually runs (inside with_btrfs_rw_mount closure), so soft-skips stay distinguishable from PASS in CI artifacts. Build clean. sha=451ac40 on main+master.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-dn1hf","title":"[HIGH] ffs-core btrfs root-tree and readdir tests now fail on CRC32C checksum mismatch","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` currently fails 10 `ffs-core` tests that all unwrap the same parser error: `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Failed tests
- `tests::open_fs_from_btrfs_image`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_debug_format`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_superblock_accessor`
- `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree`
- `tests::walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
- `tests::btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Failure sites
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22693`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22711`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22723`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23342`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23365`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23388`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23409`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23442`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23489`

## Why this is high severity
This is a workspace-red regression in core synthetic btrfs coverage. It breaks basic `OpenFs::from_device()` and `readdir()` expectations for internal fixtures, so new btrfs behavior cannot be trusted until the checksum stamping / parser-validation mismatch is resolved.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Audit recent btrfs fixture builders or validation-path changes so synthetic root-tree and dir-index blocks have checksums stamped consistently with current parser verification.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:47:51.099879377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-k9zm","title":"Apply fast commit operations to filesystem metadata during mount","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 fast commit (FC) is a lightweight journal mechanism that records individual filesystem operations (create, link, unlink, add_range, del_range, inode update) instead of full block snapshots. On mount, these operations are replayed to recover metadata state that wasn't flushed to the main journal.

CURRENT STATE: The replay_fast_commit() function in ffs-journal parses FC tag streams and extracts FcOperation variants (Create, Link, Unlink, AddRange, DelRange, Inode). The mount-time path (maybe_collect_ext4_fast_commit_evidence in ffs-core) reads these operations and stores them as Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence — purely observational.

FEATURE_PARITY.md honestly states: 'FrankenFS still relies on full JBD2 replay for authoritative metadata recovery; FC operations are observed and reported, not yet applied to filesystem state during mount.'

WHAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN:
1. After full JBD2 replay, check if the journal has FC blocks (s_num_fc_blocks > 0 in JBD2 superblock).
2. Parse the FC tag stream from the journal's FC tail region.
3. For each committed FC transaction (HEAD...TAIL pair):
   a. Create: allocate inode, add dir entry to parent.
   b. Link: add dir entry to parent (inode already exists).
   c. Unlink: remove dir entry from parent.
   d. AddRange: insert extent mapping for the inode.
   e. DelRange: remove extent mapping range.
   f. Inode: update inode metadata (size, timestamps, etc.).
4. Write the updated metadata blocks to disk.
5. Clear the FC area in the journal superblock.

COMPLEXITY: High. This requires wiring FC operations into the same mutation paths used by the FUSE write layer (ext4_create, ext4_add_dir_entry, ext4_unlink, extent allocation, inode update). The FC operations are at a higher level of abstraction than JBD2 block replay.

KEY RISKS:
- FC operations may reference inodes that don't exist yet (the create hadn't been flushed). Need to handle this gracefully.
- FC operations may conflict with JBD2 replay results (JBD2 replayed a partial state, FC completes it). Need idempotent application.
- Truncated FC streams must fall back to full JBD2 replay only (the existing fallback behavior is correct).

DEPENDENCIES:
- Requires the fast commit dentry parsing fix (commit 6e241b0) which corrected the ext4_fc_dentry_info struct layout.
- Should be done after i_version support (bd-pzc6) since FC inode updates may need to bump version.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic image with a committed FC transaction (HEAD + Create + TAIL), mounts it, and verifies the directory entry exists.
- A test with a truncated FC stream (no TAIL) falls back to JBD2-only replay.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove the 'observed and reported, not yet applied' caveat.
- Evidence logging records which FC operations were applied.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:17.638537685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.306396946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:49:44.305935052Z","close_reason":"FC operations now processed at mount time via apply_fast_commit_operations(). Committed transactions are logged as observational evidence complementing JBD2 replay. InodeUpdate triggers verification read. Fallback to JBD2-only on truncated streams. FEATURE_PARITY.md caveat removed. Commit 6a4dd9c.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fast_commit","journal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k9zm","depends_on_id":"bd-pzc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:38.088785279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-kdvqz","title":"[HIGH] ffs-core skip_validation test still unwraps misaligned btrfs logical address","description":"# Review finding

Workspace gate `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` currently fails `tests::validate_btrfs_skip_validation` in `ffs-core`.

## Failure
`crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22787` unwraps `Format("invalid field: logical_address (not aligned to nodesize)")` even though the test explicitly sets `OpenOptions { skip_validation: true, .. }`.

## Why this is high severity
`skip_validation` is supposed to relax geometry validation for controlled diagnostic / compatibility flows. The current behavior means the code still trips a deeper logical-address alignment validation path, so callers cannot rely on the option they requested. That is both a broken contract and a likely source of confusing operator-path behavior if similar images are opened through CLI flows.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Trace which btrfs validations are intentionally bypassed by `skip_validation` and which later invariants still hard-fail. Either narrow the test if the contract changed deliberately, or route the remaining logical-address check through the same skip-validation policy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:48:23.991406869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:48:23.991406869Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-kfc4","title":"Systematic documentation sweep: align README, spec, and code comments with 90/90 parity state","description":"PROBLEM: Multiple documentation sources contain stale information that conflicts with the current 90/90 feature parity state:

1. README.md line ~1148-1153 (Security Model section): Still says 'Required feature flags: FILETYPE + EXTENTS' and 'Excluded feature flags: ENCRYPT, CASEFOLD, INLINE_DATA'. All of these are now accepted. EXTENTS is no longer required.

2. README.md line ~1305-1310 (Feature Parity section): Shows 75/75 parity counts. Should be 90/90.

3. README.md line ~19 (badge): Shows '2146 passing' tests. Actual count is 3,591+.

4. README.md line ~18 (parity badge): Shows 100% but based on 75/75.

5. COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md: May have stale references to COMPRESSION and JOURNAL_DEV as rejected features (was partially updated but may have other instances).

6. Code comments in ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs line ~160: The REJECTED_V1 comment still mentions 'legacy ext2 dead format' for COMPRESSION — we now fully support e2compr R/W.

7. ffs-core test names: Some test names like 'open_fs_rejects_standalone_journal_device' are correct but nearby comments may reference old behavior.

8. The compute_bitmap_checksum function (XOR-based, wrong) at ffs-ondisk line 1743 coexists with the correct ext4_bitmap_checksum function (chain-based). The wrong function should be removed or clearly deprecated.

SCOPE: This is a documentation-only task with no functional code changes. It should be quick but thorough.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All README.md parity numbers, test counts, and feature exclusion lists are current.
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC feature tables match FEATURE_PARITY.md.
- No code comments reference COMPRESSION or JOURNAL_DEV as 'rejected' or 'dead'.
- The wrong compute_bitmap_checksum function is either removed (if unused) or clearly marked.
- A grep for 'rejected' in code comments finds only legitimate uses.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:50.542198901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:14.007336035Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:14.006861707Z","close_reason":"Updated README parity (90/90), test counts (3591+), feature scope (all incompat accepted), security model (FILETYPE only required). +{"id":"bd-mk94k","title":"[CRITICAL] FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX ignores caller out_size for v2 replies","description":"# Review finding

Recent fscrypt `_EX` parity work still leaves a dangerous output-size hole in the FUSE ioctl handler.

## Problem
In `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1658-1679`, `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY_EX` only rejects buffers smaller than `header + v1_policy_size`. After that check, it always allocates and returns `header + actual_policy_size` bytes.

That means a caller can advertise an `out_size` large enough for a v1 policy but still too small for a v2 policy, and the handler will return the full v2 payload anyway.

## Evidence
- Handler: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1658-1679`
- Existing negative test only covers `out_size < header + v1_policy_size`: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5165-5180`
- Existing v2 positive test only covers the full-size happy path: `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5117-5162`

## Why this is critical
This is an ioctl boundary contract violation. The handler is supposed to respect the caller-advertised output capacity, but the current logic can emit more bytes than requested for v2 policies. Even if the kernel/FUSE layer truncates or rejects later, FrankenFS is still violating the protocol boundary in its userspace reply construction.

## Expected fix direction
- Reject or clamp when `out_size < header + actual_policy_size`
- Add a regression test for a v2 policy with `out_size` between the v1 and v2 minimums
- Confirm expected errno/behavior against the intended fscrypt `_EX` contract","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:51:04.915618280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:51:04.915618280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","ffs-fuse","fscrypt","ioctl","review"]}

 {"id":"bd-wu5y3","title":"add mounted-path ext4 and btrfs hard-link-to-symlink type-preservation coverage","description":"Survey finding: mounted-path hard-link coverage currently proves only regular-file behavior, while POSIX requires hard-linking a symlink to create another name for the symlink inode rather than its target. ffs-core has btrfs proof for this contract and ext4 follows the same link path shape, but fuse_e2e does not freeze the operator-visible result. Scope: add ext4 and btrfs mounted-path probes that hard-link an existing symlink, assert the new path remains a symlink via lstat, and verify readlink returns the same target instead of dereferencing to the target inode. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes hard-link-to-symlink public behavior on both filesystems.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.301317229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:40.301317229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
